_comment: Atlas Horizon tile registry.
TOP-LEVEL SECTIONS
environments  – slug → display name for every supported environment.
gamemodes     – slug → display name for every supported gamemode.
tiles         – array of tile definitions (see TILE FIELDS below).
TILE FIELDS
id              – Numeric tile ID stored in saved map data. Never change after first use.
key             – Unique string identifier (e.g. 'wall-1').
name            – Human-readable display name.
category        – Palette grouping.
size            – 1 for 1×1, 2 for 2×2 (origin at top-left).
wall            – true = treated as a solid wall for validation.
zIndex          – Visual layering (higher = drawn on top).
fallbackColor   – CSS colour used when no image is available.
showInGamemode  – null = always visible in palette.
string[] = only when map gamemode slug is listed.
showInEnvironment – null = always visible.
string[] = only when map environment slug is listed.
defaultImage    – Global image path used when no env override matches.
null = tile always needs an env override.
defaultWidth    – Render width in cell units.  null = auto (naturalWidth / 100).
defaultHeight   – Render height in cell units. null = auto (naturalHeight / 100).
defaultOffsetX  – X offset in cells.  null = auto (centred within size).
defaultOffsetY  – Y offset in cells.  null = auto (bottom-aligned).
defaultOpacity  – Image opacity 0–1.
environmentOverrides – env-slug → override object.
Only include entries where an image EXISTS for that environment.
An absent entry means 'no specific image; use defaultImage or cross-env fallback'.
Override object fields (all optional except 'image'):
image   – path string or null (explicit no-image for this env).
width, height, offsetX, offsetY – override auto-compute for this env only.
opacity – overrides defaultOpacity.
mirrorPair – id of the complementary tile for mirror-mode swapping (optional).
